mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal shows no statistical significance against a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC over any 2-year window, or housing starts decline while consumption rises without affecting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic momentum and triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns during early-cycle phases or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative rank IC over rolling 3-year periods or fails to predict returns when payroll growth is concentrated in low-wage sectors.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return shows no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates or produces negative returns in >50% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which signals economic slowdown and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when declines are driven by temporary weather disruptions or the 20-day forward return remains positive in >60% of triggered cases.
